title: pseudolycopodiella benjaminiana
intro: Pseudolycopodiella benjaminiana, also known as the Benjamin's spike moss, is a species of lycopodiaceous plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pseudolycopodiella benjaminiana include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. Pseudolycopodiella benjaminiana can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be under the guidance of a medical professional due to limited research and potential side effects.
